extra.reasoning: The property NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is VIOLATED. Two rules share the same trigger condition (hyperkalemia) but write different outputs: rec_a_from_conjunction sets rec_drug_a := true and rec_b_from_trigger sets rec_drug_b := true. Priority-based conflict resolution applies per-output (highest-priority matching rule per output wins), so it never causes these two rules to compete — each is the sole rule for its own output. When hyperkalemia=true, both rules fire, making rec_drug_a=true AND rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, which makes the mutual-exclusion property false. The elderly input is irrelevant (it only affects rec_drug_c); set elderly=false for a minimal witness. Witness {hyperkalemia:true, elderly:false}: rec_drug_a=true (rec_a_from_conjunction), rec_drug_b=true (rec_b_from_trigger), rec_drug_c=false (default, elderly not set). rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b = true -> violation.
